events.title: SCTLD coral disease outbreak
events.description: Stony coral tissue loss disease has affected over half of Florida's reef-building coral species. Brain corals, star corals, and pillar corals have been hit hardest. Active treatment programs are ongoing at major sites.

events.title: Agricultural runoff from south Florida
events.description: Nutrient-laden freshwater from sugar plantations and the Everglades chronically degrades reef-flat water clarity, especially after summer rains.

divingImpactNote: Choose offshore sites and wall dives over inshore patch reefs for the best visibility. Many operators participate in coral-restoration nurseries — ask about joining a fragment-outplanting dive.

events.title: Sargassum influx
events.description: Massive seasonal sargassum blooms in the Mexican Caribbean choke beaches and reduce surface visibility. Worst in summer; underwater visibility on the wall is largely unaffected.

events.title: SCTLD coral disease outbreak
events.description: Mesoamerican Reef SCTLD outbreak has caused significant coral mortality. Active treatment dive programs operate at some sites.

divingImpactNote: Visibility on the wall remains excellent year-round. Avoid June–August if surface conditions matter — beach-level sargassum can be heavy. Support operators participating in SCTLD treatment dives.

events.title: SCTLD coral disease outbreak
events.description: Bonaire's SCTLD outbreak has affected over 30 coral species. STINAPA monitors and treats affected colonies; recovery is patchy.

events.title: Coastal-development runoff
events.description: New resort and housing construction on the leeward coast has increased sediment runoff at a handful of shore-dive sites.

divingImpactNote: Despite SCTLD, Bonaire shore diving remains world-class — fish life and topography are intact. Pay the STINAPA tag; it directly funds disease treatment.

events.title: SCTLD coral disease outbreak
events.description: Bay Islands SCTLD outbreak progressing. Treatment programs are smaller-scale than in Florida.

events.title: Plastic pollution from Río Motagua
events.description: Honduran-Guatemalan river discharge brings significant plastic and debris into Caribbean currents. Visible on north-coast beaches after storms.

divingImpactNote: Wall diving on the south side stays clear. Choose operators that participate in shoreline cleanups.

events.title: SCTLD coral disease outbreak
events.description: Same Bay Islands SCTLD progression as Roatan. Hard-coral loss is visible at most reef sites.

events.title: Plastic pollution from Río Motagua
events.description: Caribbean current brings significant plastic debris through the Bay Islands.

divingImpactNote: Whale shark season (Mar–Apr) is unaffected — they're plankton feeders cruising the channel. For reef diving, choose deeper sites where impact is lower.

events.title: Agricultural runoff from Queensland
events.description: Inshore Great Barrier Reef receives runoff from sugarcane and banana plantations, especially after wet-season rains. Northern outer-reef sites (where Cod Hole sits) are largely buffered.

events.title: Crown-of-thorns starfish outbreaks
events.description: Cyclical COTS outbreaks driven partly by nutrient runoff. AIMS-led culling teams operate at affected reefs.

divingImpactNote: Outer Ribbon Reef sites are buffered from coastal pressures and remain in good shape. Inshore-reef trips are more affected by runoff and clarity issues.

events.title: Coral disease — yellow band + white syndrome
events.description: Recurrent disease outbreaks combined with heavy diver-training load have eroded shallow-reef cover near Sairee Beach.

events.title: Wastewater issues
events.description: Island wastewater infrastructure is at capacity during peak season; nutrient-loading effects visible at some shore-entry sites.

divingImpactNote: Pick dive shops with ecology-focused training and choose deeper offshore sites over the heavily-trained shallow shore reefs.

events.title: Heavy thermal stress + coral mortality
events.description: Severe 2024 bleaching event triggered closure of large parts of Hon Mun MPA to diving for restoration work. Check current status with operators.

divingImpactNote: Hon Mun MPA access has been restricted since 2024 — verify operator permits before booking. Consider Phu Quoc as an alternative.

events.title: Marine heatwave gorgonian die-off
events.description: Successive Mediterranean marine heatwaves have caused mass mortality among red gorgonians in shallow water. Deeper colonies (>30 m) survived better.

divingImpactNote: Shallow gorgonian gardens have thinned considerably. Go deeper (>25 m) to see colonies that survived the heatwaves.

events.title: Marine heatwave thermal stress
events.description: Mediterranean marine heatwaves have affected gorgonian and sponge communities. Cave and wreck diving largely unaffected.

divingImpactNote: Limestone caves, wrecks, and arches dominate diving here — these are unaffected by water-quality issues. Open-water reef dives feel the heat impact.

events.title: Severe 2024 bleaching event
events.description: Extreme thermal stress in 2024 caused widespread coral bleaching across the Maldives. Lagoon and shallow reef-flat impact has been worst.

events.title: Resort sand-mining and dredging
events.description: Resort construction frequently involves channel dredging and beach replenishment, with chronic localised water-clarity impacts.

divingImpactNote: Pelagic encounters (manta, whale shark, hammerhead) are unaffected — go for those over coral-cover trips. Deeper thila and kandu (channel dives) retain better hard-coral cover than reef flats.

events.title: Coral mortality and declining shark/ray populations
events.description: Tofo's once-iconic whale shark numbers crashed in the 2010s. Likely combination of artisanal-fishing pressure, gillnetting bycatch, and changing currents.

divingImpactNote: Whale shark season (Oct–Mar) is still on but encounters are less reliable than a decade ago. Manta sightings remain consistent at Manta Reef and other cleaning stations.

events.title: 2024 marine heatwave bleaching
events.description: Sustained high SSTs caused significant bleaching on Ningaloo's lagoon reefs. Outer-reef bommies fared better.

divingImpactNote: Whale shark, manta, and humpback seasons are largely unaffected — they're seasonal pelagic encounters. For reef diving, focus on outer-reef sites over lagoon flats.

events.title: 2024 bleaching event + invasive algae
events.description: West Hawaii experienced significant 2024 bleaching plus expanding alien algae (Avrainvillea amadelpha) competing with native coral.

divingImpactNote: Manta night dive and blackwater dives are unaffected by reef condition. Day-diving over hard reef now shows recovery in patches.

events.title: 2024 bleaching event
events.description: April 2024 surveys recorded localised bleaching on shallow reef flats; submerged slopes largely unaffected.

divingImpactNote: Reef slopes from 10 m down are still vivid. Liveaboard-only season (Mar–Jun) is short — book early.

events.title: Plastic from regional currents
events.description: Indo-Pacific currents bring substantial plastic debris through the Raja Ampat archipelago — visible on remote-island beaches and in surface drift lines.

divingImpactNote: Underwater visibility and reef condition remain world-class. Surface plastic is a visible problem — operators run frequent cleanups and welcome volunteers.

events.title: Sargassum influx
events.description: Atlantic sargassum blooms wash onto Belizean shorelines and into the atoll. Underwater impact at the Blue Hole itself is limited.

divingImpactNote: Blue Hole proper sees little impact — it's a deep geological feature. Surrounding atoll reefs are affected. Trip-cost includes Marine Reserve fee.

events.title: Industrial fishing bycatch on shark populations
events.description: Sardine fishery in adjacent waters affects bycatch of tiger sharks, ragged-tooth sharks, and other Aliwal Shoal apex predators. Park enforces strict no-fish zones.

divingImpactNote: Sardine Run remains world-class. Tiger shark dives are unchanged — choose operators participating in shark-tagging research.

events.title: Multiple bleaching events (1998, 2016, 2024)
events.description: Granitic Seychelles has been hit by three major bleaching events since 1998. Cumulative coral loss is significant.

divingImpactNote: Pelagic-focused trips (whale shark, mola mola at L'Ilot) remain strong. Reef diving has thinned but unique granitic boulder topography keeps it visually striking.

events.title: Multiple bleaching events (1998, 2016, 2024)
events.description: Same regional bleaching pattern as Mahe. Inner Seychelles reefs have lost majority of hard coral.

divingImpactNote: Big-fish diving (whale shark, ray aggregations) remains the draw. Pelagic itineraries skip the worst reef-loss areas.

events.title: Reef thinning from cumulative bleaching
events.description: Zanzibar's marquee reef has lost substantial cover through the 2016 + 2020 + 2024 bleaching cycle. Dolphin and turtle encounters remain consistent.

divingImpactNote: Manta and turtle encounters off Mnemba are still excellent — they don't depend on coral cover. Skip if hard-coral landscapes are what you came for.

events.title: Chronic agricultural runoff + warming
events.description: Bocas reefs have declined steadily for two decades due to banana-plantation runoff, sedimentation, and recurrent bleaching. SCTLD now adding pressure.

divingImpactNote: Reef diving here is patchy. Worth visiting for macro/critter diving, sloths, and the broader Caribbean-coast culture rather than dramatic coral scenery.

events.title: Cumulative bleaching damage
events.description: Similan reefs hit by 2010, 2016, and 2024 bleaching cycles. Soft coral and gorgonian recovery is patchy. Park closed seasonally (May–Oct) to allow recovery.

divingImpactNote: Granitic boulder topography is unchanged and still stunning. Pelagic encounters (whale shark, manta) are the headline. Liveaboard-only Nov–Apr.
